description: A rounded container made of clay or metal, used for cooking or storage. 'Tomi' names earth transformed—clay shaped by hands, fired to permanence.
sound_symbolism: The word shapes earth. 'To' defines with clear form, pot taking shape. Then 'mi' holds with intimate care, vessel for daily nourishment.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo tomi' (the pot). Typically ceramic or earthen. Related to 'nuroa' (kitchen) where pots do their work.

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Shape following function—the pot's curve holding heat, pouring smoothly, pleasing the hand.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Pottery as transformation—earth, water, fire combining to create lasting form.
